changes.motivation: Create scaffolding for a new 'Effective Sandboxing: Live Dangerously, Safely' presentation
changes.summary: Created `src/content/presentations/effective-sandboxing/` directory with a `01-title.mdx` title slide (slideLayout: center, author: Jack Langston) and an `images/` subdirectory.

changes.motivation: Add author bio slide matching the existing hype-velocity presentation style
changes.summary: Created `02-author.mdx` by copying the author slide from `hype-velocity` and copying `images/headshot.jpg`. Updated author name to Jack Langston in `01-title.mdx`.

changes.motivation: Build out presentation content slides covering the talk structure
changes.summary: Created slides 03–10 covering: What is 'Dangerous', Why live dangerously, and six Issues slides (Unintended Edits, Secrets Env/Files/Other processes, Supply chain attacks, Unintended communication). 'Dangerous' and 'Dangerously' styled with `#f97066` (error-400 red italic).

changes.motivation: Add an Ideal Solution criteria checklist and option comparison slides
changes.summary: Created `11-solution-sandboxing.mdx` (Ideal Solution) and slides 12–15 (Option: Agent-provided, Containers/VMs, Bubblewrap/sandbox-exec, Nono) each with a 9-item two-column checklist using inline SVG checkboxes. Items: secrets access, network deny-by-default, filesystem controls, concurrent agents, host utilities, audit log, profiles, strong enforcement, cross-platform, FOSS, easy to use, easy to share.

changes.motivation: Mark each checklist option slide with red/yellow/green indicators to show how well each option meets criteria
changes.summary: Each option slide uses inline SVG marks: ✗ (red `#f97066`), ✓ (green `#32d583`), − (yellow `#fdb022`) per bullet. Slides 12–16 wrapped with `fontSize: '0.75em'` to prevent overflow.

changes.motivation: Add a Nono-specific solution slide showing all criteria met
changes.summary: Created `16-solution-sandboxing-nono.mdx` with all checklist items set to green checkmarks and a footnote: 'Nono is a custom sandboxing tool built on top of bubblewrap and ebpf. Learn more at nono.panfactum.com'.

changes.motivation: Add background education slides about the underlying technologies Nono uses
changes.summary: Created `17-bubblewrap.mdx` and `18-ebpf.mdx` as placeholder slides covering Bubblewrap and eBPF respectively.

changes.motivation: Explain Nono's architecture and CLI commands
changes.summary: Created `19-nono-architecture.mdx` with a two-column layout: left column lists core concepts (Linux Namespaces, cgroups, eBPF Network Hooks, Secret Injection, Overlay Filesystems), right column lists Nono CLI commands (nono run, nono profile create, nono exec, nono log).

changes.motivation: Deep-dive slides for each major Nono feature with code examples
changes.summary: Created slides 20–24 (Nono: Profile, Secret Bindings, Network Policy, Filesystem Access, Audit Log) each with a two-column layout: left column 'How it works' bullets, right column code block with `data-trim data-noescape` for Reveal.js highlight.js syntax highlighting.

changes.motivation: Demonstrate Nono live during the talk
changes.summary: Created `25-nono-demo.mdx` as a demo slide placeholder.

changes.motivation: Show organizational benefits across different stakeholder roles
changes.summary: Created `26-benefits-org.mdx` with four columns (Developer, Operator, Security, Exec) each with a relevant SVG icon and three bullet points.

changes.motivation: Summarize key takeaways with visual emphasis on critical words
changes.summary: Created `27-takeaways.mdx` with four bullets. 'dangerous' and 'most' colored red (`#f97066`), 'required' and 'bridges' colored green (`#32d583`).

changes.motivation: Provide a call-to-action slide with links to Nono resources
changes.summary: Created `28-get-started.mdx` with links to github.com/panfactum/nono and panfactum.com/presentations/effective-sandboxing, plus a brief description of Nono.

changes.motivation: Add a closing thank-you slide with social links and icons
changes.summary: Created `29-thank-you.mdx` with four social links (LinkedIn, GitHub, panfactum.com, nono.panfactum.com) using SVG icon paths extracted from `@iconify/json/simple-icons` for LinkedIn and GitHub. Icons sized `1.2em` with `vertical-align: middle`.

changes.motivation: Implement a footnote system so slides can display contextual notes below the presentation frame
changes.summary: Added optional `footnote` field to the presentation slide schema in `src/content/config.ts`. Updated `[slug].astro` to extract footnotes from slide frontmatter and pass them as an indexed array to `PresentationViewer`. Updated `PresentationViewer.tsx` to listen to `slidechanged` events and render the current footnote below the `.reveal` container with `min-h-[3rem]` always reserved. Footnote area is hidden in fullscreen mode.

changes.motivation: Prevent long slide titles from wrapping to a second line
changes.summary: Added `container-type: inline-size` to `.reveal .slides section` and set `h2` font-size to `clamp(0.8em, 3.5cqi, 1.8em)` in `reveal-panfactum-theme.css` so headings shrink dynamically to fit one line.

changes.motivation: Align checkbox SVG icons with the first line of wrapped text
changes.summary: Added `marginTop: '0.3em'` to SVG icons in checklist items and `marginBottom: '0.6em'` for spacing between items. Used `alignItems: 'flex-start'` on the row container.

problems: CSS custom properties (Tailwind variables like `var(--color-error-300)`) do not resolve in Reveal.js MDX inline styles — the agent had to discover this by user feedback and fall back to hardcoded hex values. This should be documented in the presentation authoring guide.

The `pnpm check` TypeScript errors during the footnote feature implementation were caused by the Astro content collection schema not declaring the `footnote` field. The schema file (`src/content/config.ts`) had no documentation indicating that all frontmatter fields used in slides must be registered there first.

Reveal.js initialization has a race condition on dev server cold start — the first page load sometimes shows an empty slide area because the Reveal module hasn't finished compiling. This is a known-but-undocumented dev environment behavior.

The `@iconify/json` package icon body strings use a `0 0 24 24` viewBox but the package has no README or helper API — the correct extraction method (using Python to parse the JSON) had to be discovered manually.

Checklist slides 12–16 overflowed the slide area because the two-column 9-item layout has no automatic font scaling. The fix (wrapping with `fontSize: '0.75em'`) works but is a manual per-slide workaround; the presentation system has no built-in overflow handling or content-fit scaling for non-code slides.
